title: Path to Prove Connectedness via Continuous Functions
description: A visual proof strategy demonstrating how a continuous function with a connected codomain implies a connected domain, a key concept for continuous internal evaluation.
elements: Separation of X (U, V) (Assumption for contradiction)
Contradiction: f(U) and f(V) separate Y
relationships: f is a continuous function from X to a connected space Y
Assume, for contradiction, that X is disconnected into two non-empty, open, disjoint sets U and V
The images f(U) and f(V) must be open because f is continuous and U/V are open
f(U) and f(V) are non-empty, disjoint, and their union is Y, which would mean Y is disconnected
This contradicts the given fact that Y is connected, therefore the initial assumption that X is disconnected must be false
